title: Crocodile Watches the Pieces Move
description: On pages 5–8, this part focuses on crocodile watches the pieces move, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: Crocodile Watches the Pieces Move. The Straw Hats are trapped beneath Rain Dinners as Crocodile prepares to drown them and erase the kingdom’s last hope. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: You should know first-hand just how much the king cares for his subjects...!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: ...Only the old folks still say that.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: I personally don't care for him at all.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: It wasn't for the king's sake,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: That I decided to come here to Yuba and help you, dad.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Ohhh!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Did you hear the rebels mow number over 100,000...?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Yeah, I heard...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: There's been lots of casualties too...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Hmph, don't listen to the nay-sayers.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Haha...

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Their doubts will easily be washed away once the rain comes...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Don't retreat!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: This is terrible!

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: The irrigation canals have been destroyed!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: Who would've done such a thing!?

dialogue.speaker: Tashigi
dialogue.text: Now the town's cut off from water!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: WAHA!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: If only the rain would come...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: I hear Erumalu's a ghost town now.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: It's already been over a year since it last rained...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Don't lose faith, Kouza.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: The rain will come.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: This man is critically wounded! He needs treatment at once!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I don't want to doubt you but the fact is, there are people starving out there!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: So whether or not you've actually used it in the past, I'm sure you have some left to spare!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Absolutely not! We must wait for the rain to fall naturally!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Dance Powder is a powder that calls forth misfortune.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Its use is prohibited!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Alabasta is not the only country that needs rain.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I'm sure using just a little won't hurt!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: If we could just make a little bit of rain... Just a small amount enough to get the people through this drought... Then the rebellion might stop!

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Give us our rain back, King cobra!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Slump...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: That would only exacerbate the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: You're undergo-timating the dangers of Dance Powder, Kouza...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I won't stand by and watch the towns dry up one by one anymore!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You haven't changed at all, have you...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: No, I've changed...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I can fight mow!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Just you watch! I'll come back to steal your rain!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Why would you do such a pointless thing...!?

summary: This part develops crocodile watches the pieces move and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
